commit.message:
docs: add tool bridge plan — provider abstraction & lazy registration

Define IToolProvider, IToolCatalog, IToolRegistrar, IToolExpander interfaces
for bridging external tool sources (MCPorter, cron, bundled) onto Copilot SDK
sessions. Key design decisions:

- Lazy registration: only default tools on session creation, expand_tools
  adds more via ResumeSessionAsync (no AvailableTools/ExcludedTools needed,
  CLI built-in tools stay visible)
- Hybrid expand_tools: load mode (provider/tool names) + query mode
  (keyword search without loading) for semantic discovery
- ISP-compliant interface split: catalog (read) / registrar (write) /
  expander (session-aware)
- Async pull model for surface change notifications
- Same-tier collision = hard error, teardown contract documented
- Scalability validated via SDK source analysis (~100-200 tools ceiling)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address all PR review comments on GitRepositoryService

- Let GitHubRateLimitException propagate through SyncRepositoryAsync and
  SyncGitHubRepositoryAsync so the controller can return HTTP 429
- Fix case-sensitive github.com provider detection to use OrdinalIgnoreCase
- Dispose HttpResponseMessage at all three ExecuteWithRetryAsync call sites

- Extract X-RateLimit-Reset header value before disposing the response,
  then throw GitHubRateLimitException cleanly without accessing a
  disposed object
- Replace raw string slicing in ParseGitHubUrl with Uri.TryCreate for
  HTTPS URLs so query strings and fragments are ignored via AbsolutePath;
  SSH URLs now explicitly strip ? and # characters before parsing

* fix: MaxAttempts rename, strict URL validation, ArgumentException for invalid URL, JsonPropertyName for DownloadUrl

- SyncRepositoryAsync throws ArgumentException for invalid URL instead

- ParseGitHubUrl: HTTPS-only, exactly 2 path segments, reject non-empty
  query/fragment; SSH path returns ("","") immediately on ? or # chars
- Update BadRequest message to include SSH URL format example

  directly

commit.message:
Fix blocking execSync during app quit process (#704)

Fixes CQ-23: `applyUpdateOnQuit` was blocking the event loop during
`app.quit` with synchronous child process calls, causing the app to
potentially hang for up to 60 seconds (macOS) or 300 seconds (Windows).

## Changes Made

### macOS (`execSync` → detached shell script)
- Moved the blocking `execSync('unzip ...')` call out of the main
process and into the detached shell script generated by
`buildMacQuitUpdateScript`.
- The shell script now handles the full update lifecycle after the app
exits: extracting the ZIP, discovering the `.app` bundle, replacing the
old app, and cleaning up temp files.
- If extraction fails (e.g., no `.app` found), the script cleans up and
exits gracefully.

### Windows (`execFileSync` → detached `spawn`)
- Replaced the blocking `execFileSync(Update.exe, ['--update', ...])`
call (with a 300-second timeout) with a detached `spawn()` call.
- `Update.exe` now runs independently after the app exits, with error
event handling to log spawn failures.

### Tests
- Updated integration tests (macOS-only) to create real zip files for
testing the new script behavior.
- Updated unit tests for the new `buildMacQuitUpdateScript` signature,
with new tests covering unzip inclusion, `.app` discovery, and failure
abort logic.

## Testing

- ✅ All 1946 main process tests pass
- ✅ TypeScript compilation passes
- ✅ CodeQL security scan reports 0 alerts

state.

commit.message:
fix(setup): eliminate TOCTOU race condition in project ID file write (#285)

* Initial plan

* fix(security): eliminate TOCTOU race in project-id writeFile (CWE-367)

Replace the check-then-write pattern with an atomic O_CREAT|O_EXCL
write by using the 'wx' flag in fs.writeFile. This closes the window
between the earlier readFile existence check (line 41) and the
subsequent write (previously line 95) where an attacker or concurrent
process could modify the file.

- fs.writeFile(..., {encoding: 'utf8', flag: 'wx'}) replaces plain
  string encoding arg; 'wx' maps to O_WRONLY | O_CREAT | O_EXCL
- EEXIST is now caught and logged at debug level (expected in concurrent
  runs; value is deterministic so no action needed)
- All other write errors continue to log at warning level and proceed
- New test 'handles concurrent write (EEXIST from wx flag) gracefully'
  exercises the EEXIST code path by pre-creating the file with invalid
  content so the initial readFile falls through, then verifying the
  function returns the generated ID without propagating EEXIST

Resolves: CodeQL js/file-system-race (high severity, CWE-367)

commit.message:
Fix cleanupWatchesForWindow to implement cleanup logic (#691)

Implements the actual cleanup logic for `cleanupWatchesForWindow` which
was previously a no-op that used `void` expressions to suppress lint
warnings. File watchers were never cleaned up when windows closed,
causing resource leaks (file handles, memory, CPU from unnecessary
change events).

## Changes Made

- **`webContentsId` tracking**: Added `webContentsId: number` field to
the `WatchEntry` interface so each watch records which window's
`webContents` created it.
- **`startWatch` update**: Now stores `sender.id` on each `WatchEntry`
when a watch is created.
- **`cleanupWatchesForWindow` implementation**: Iterates over active
watches and calls `stopWatch` on entries whose `webContentsId` matches
the closing window's `webContents.id`, properly closing the watcher,
clearing debounce timers, and removing the entry from the map.
- **Tests added**: New test file
`src/main/services/file-watch-service.test.ts` with 7 tests covering
cleanup for matching windows, preservation of other windows' watches,
no-op edge cases, `stopWatch`, and `stopAllWatches`.

## Testing

- ✅ All 84 main test files (1951 tests) pass
- ✅ Code review — no issues found
- ✅ CodeQL security scan — no alerts

commit.message:
fix: auto-end turn only when player had no playable cards before drawing (#1)

Refined the auto-end logic to respect player strategy:

- If player had NO playable cards before drawing (forced draw) AND the
  drawn card is also not playable → turn ends automatically.
- If player chose to draw despite having playable cards (strategy) → they
  keep their turn and can play an existing card or end manually.

This preserves the strategic option of drawing even when a play is
available while still removing friction for forced draws.

Fixes #1

* feat(ui): SSE streaming, dark mode, PWA, persisted settings, auto-copy

Significant changes:

- **SSE streaming transcription** (`api/transcribe.ts`): Replaced the single-shot fetch + Promise approach with a full Server-Sent Events streaming client (`transcribeFileSSE`). The API endpoint changed from `/transcribe` to `/transcribe/stream`. Progress events (`stage`, `message`) are now fired in real-time for each pipeline stage, enabling live UI feedback during long transcriptions.

- **Persisted user settings** (`context/TranscriberContext.tsx`): All user preferences (diarize, autoCopy, transcriptCollapsed, showDropzone, darkMode) are now stored in `localStorage` under `transcriber-settings` and restored on page load. Previously all settings were lost on refresh.

- **Dark / light mode** (`App.tsx`, `amplify-overwrite.css`): Full dark-mode support via Amplify `createTheme` + `defaultDarkModeOverride`. Preference is persisted. New sticky app header added with a sun/moon toggle and a GitHub repository link.

- **PWA installability** (`vite.config.ts`, `package.json`, `public/icons/`): Added `vite-plugin-pwa` with a Workbox service worker (no precaching — app requires backend) and a Web App Manifest. App can now be installed as a standalone web app on desktop and mobile. API dev-proxy target updated from port 8000 → 8080.

- **Auto-copy transcript** (`Controls.tsx`, `TranscriberContext.tsx`): New "Auto-copy" option — when enabled, the completed plain-text transcript is automatically copied to the clipboard via `copyToClipboard(toPlainText(result))` on transcription completion.

- **Controls component extracted** (`components/Controls.tsx`): The inline `Controls` function previously defined in `App.tsx` was pulled out into its own component and redesigned. Now exposes Diarize, Auto-copy, and Upload-file toggles with hover tooltips, and ties `isRecording` state to disable Reset while recording.

- **Collapsible transcript panel** (`TranscriptDisplay.tsx`): Added a collapse/expand chevron to the transcript card header. Collapsed state is persisted in settings. When collapsed the card shrinks to just the header bar, freeing vertical space.

- **File size validation** (`AudioUpload.tsx`): Added a 50 MB hard cap on uploaded files with an inline dismissible `Alert` error, preventing  oversized uploads from being silently passed to the server.

- **Centralised types** (`types.ts`): `Status`, `TranscriptView`, `TranscriberState`, `PersistedSettings`, and all SSE event interfaces (`SSEProgressEvent`, `SSECompleteEvent`, `SSEErrorEvent`, `SSECallbacks`) moved to a single `types.ts` module instead of being scattered across context and API files.

* feat(server): reverse-proxy prefix support, UI re-mount at root, PWA serving

- **Configurable URL prefix** (`server/app.py`): `create_app()` gains a `prefix` argument that scopes all API routes and the UI under an arbitrary path (e.g. `/ai/transcribe`). Exposed as `--prefix` CLI flag and `TRANSCRIBER_PREFIX` env var. Uvicorn is started with `root_path` set to the same prefix so `request.base_url` reflects the external URL correctly behind reverse proxies.

- **UI re-mounted at app root** (`server/ui.py`): The UI was previously served at `/ui`. It now serves at `{prefix}/` (i.e. the application root). Static assets moved from `{prefix}/ui/...` to `{prefix}/static/...`. `index.html` is rewritten at serve time with a  regex that converts Vite's relative `./subdir/file` asset paths to absolute `{prefix}/static/subdir/file` paths, so the SPA resolves assets  correctly under any prefix without a Vite rebuild.

- **PWA file serving** (`server/ui.py` — `_mount_pwa_files`): New helper registers dedicated FastAPI routes for every root-level PWA file emitted by `vite-plugin-pwa` (`sw.js`, `manifest.webmanifest`, `workbox-*.js`).
  `sw.js` is served with a `Service-Worker-Allowed` header scoped to `{prefix}/`. Workbox runtime chunks are served with a 1-year immutable cache. These files are intentionally NOT under `/static/` because the service worker scope requires them at the root path.

- **CI workflow granularity** (`.github/workflows/check.yml`): The single `just check` step was split into four separate named steps — Lint, Type-check, Security, and Audit — so failures are pinpointed immediately in the GitHub Actions UI without reading logs.

- **UI workflow uses `just`** (`.github/workflows/ui.yml`, `tasks/ui.just`): All UI CI steps now delegate to `just ui-*` recipes instead of running `npm` directly from the workflow. The `just` recipes themselves were also normalised to use `npm --prefix ui` instead of `cd ui && npm`, making them runnable from the repo root.

- **Test coverage for new behaviour** (`tests/test_ui.py`): Added `test_asset_paths_rewritten_to_static` to assert that the at-serve-time path rewriting works; updated all existing UI tests to reflect the move from `/ui` → `/` and `/ui/css` → `/static/css`.

* ci(ui): use npm ci for dependency installation in GitHub Actions

* feat(client): add verify_ssl param to REST, SSE, and WebSocket senders

Allows skipping TLS certificate validation (verify_ssl=False) for
self-signed or internal server certificates. Also updates README and
UI docs, adds demo screenshot, and migrates pre-commit stage names.

commit.message:
Carving experience: custom cursors, pressure sensitivity, quick undo

Custom cursors & width indicator:
- SVG data URI cursors per tool: Fine (╱), V Gouge (∨), U Gouge (∪)
- Erase mode gets circle-X cursor
- Tool-specific CSS classes applied via updateToolCursorClass()
- Dashed ring follows cursor showing tool width at current zoom
- Ring responds to live pen pressure (wider when pressing harder)
- Ring hides during active stroke drawing, shows on hover

Pressure-sensitive carving:
- Reads PointerEvent.pressure (0-1) and tilt per point during capture
- Stored as {x, y, pressure, tilt} in stroke data (backward compat)
- carveStrokeRenderData() modulates width and opacity by pressure
- Mouse (0.5) renders identically to before; pen varies 0.4x to 1.6x
- Light pressure = thinner/lighter cuts, heavy = wider/deeper

Quick stroke undo:
- Z key in carve/erase mode removes last stroke (no Ctrl needed)
- Shift+Z restores it from strokeRedoBuffer
- Buffer clears on mode change for safety
- Works for both element strokes and background strokes

- Update npm-publish skill with mandatory 3-step post-publish verification

Root cause: platform package.json versions were not bumped during the 0.1.14
release, so CI tried to re-publish 0.1.13 (already existed), npm rejected with
E403, and || true swallowed the errors. The main duroxide@0.1.14 was published
without any platform binaries.

feat: daemon session recovery + headless default + multi-session routing

- Daemon auto-recovers stale sessions: get_or_create_session now
  health-checks existing sessions via page.url() and re-launches
  Chrome when the browser died (e.g. after `session close`)

  command, preventing stale WS URLs on reconnect

- Active session tracking via /tmp/onecrawl-active-session marker file
  so navigate/eval/etc. automatically target the correct named session

commit.message:
feat: multi-theme picker (5 palettes × light/dark)

Replace the single dark/light toggle with a palette picker button
that opens a popover with:

- 5 colour swatches: Indigo (default), Ocean, Forest, Rose, Amber

chosen palette are persisted separately in localStorage
